text: 261665 1149996 FLORA OF CAMP ROBERTS, CALIFORNIA Plagiobothrys tenellus (Nutt.) A. Gray Determined by: R. Kelley 11 July 2002 SAN LUIS OBISPO CO.: Camp Roberts. Above palisades above Boy Scout Road, south of Nacimiento River. UTMs (NAD 27): zone 10, 694774 E, 3959133 N Elevation 260 m. Ascending annual, 3-20 cm tall; corolla white. Scattered, ca. 200 plants, with Quercus douglasii, Adenostoma fasciculatum, Bromus hordeaceus, Plagiobothrys nothofulvus, Erodium spp. M. Wetherwax & E.L. Painter CR-147 20 March 2001 with J. Greenhouse

Calystegia malacophylla (Greene) Munz subsp. pedicellata (Jepson) Munz det. R.K. Brummitt

Fort Hunter Liggett (Training Area 26), Steve Creek and feeder drainages, to confluence with Nacimiento River, ca. 5 air km NW of Crystal Knob, ca. 5 air km WSW of Sycamore Reservoir UTMs (NAD 27, map estimate): zone 10S, 662650E, 3968100N moderate to steep slopes gravelly to sandy loam 320 m

Associates: Quercus, Acer macrophyllum, Ceanothus cuneatus, Toxicodendron diversilobum

Locally common. Perennial from deep-seated rhizome. Sprawling, branches to 5 dm long. Very pubescent. Corolla white, aging yellowish.

P. Douglas, N. Hastings, R. Smith, E. Painter, E. Neese, C. Popolizio HL 1152 1 April 1995

Plants of San Bernardino Co., California, USA Euphorbiaceae

Ditaxis neomexicana (Muell. Arg.) A. A. Heller

Southern foot of Chemehuevi Mountains: Low rolling hills north of Havasu Lake Road, along powerline (road 056). UTM Zone 11S; 3820912N 0722388E. NAD83 Alt.: 478m/1569ft. Narrow wash crossing road: silty pocket on north side of road where water collected. With Acacia greggii, Asclepias subulata, Encelia farinosa, Larrea tridentata, Fouquieria splendens, Bebbia juncea aspera. BLM permit 6850(CA-930)P.

Annual or young perennial. Locally abundant. Sarah J. De Groot 6795 w/ Amber Swanson, Jay Sullivan 2 Sep 2011
associatedTaxa: With Acacia greggii, Asclepias subulata, Encelia farinosa, Larrea tridentata, Fouquieria splendens, Bebbia juncea aspera.
locality: Southern foot of Chemehuevi Mountains: Low rolling hills north of Havasu Lake Road, along powerline (road 056).
habitat: Narrow wash crossing road: silty pocket on north side of road where water collected.
